## Summary

<!-- Describe the changes you made and why -->

**Fixes:** #1

**Area:** `pkg/provider/file`

**Problem:** Under high-frequency consecutive writes to watched configuration files, the Traefik File Provider could stop processing and applying dynamic configuration updates, entering a deadlocked or stalled state.

**Solution:** Implemented a robust file-watching provider using fsnotify with:
- Debounce mechanism (configurable, default 500ms) to coalesce rapid consecutive writes
- Content-based change detection (SHA-256 hash comparison) to filter spurious events
- Atomic write pattern support via debounced event handling
- Thread-safe operations with proper mutex synchronization

## Required Validation

- [ ] `python3 build.py` executed successfully
- [ ] Diagnostic artifact included: `diagnostic/build-*.logd`
- [ ] Diagnostic artifact included: `diagnostic/build-*.json`

## Diagnostic Artifacts

<!-- These are generated by build.py -->

## Checklist

- [ ] The File Provider reliably detects and applies configuration changes under high-frequency consecutive writes
- [ ] The file watcher does not enter a deadlocked or stalled state
- [ ] Atomic write patterns are fully supported without losing the active file watch
- [ ] Go tests pass
- [ ] `go build` succeeds
